ogTitle: Pikes Creek vs Pile Gully: Climate Safety

heading: Pikes Creek vs Pile Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pikes Creek, Queensland and Pile Gully, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pile Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Pikes Creek (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pikes Creek or Pile Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pikes Creek has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Pile Gully scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pikes Creek scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Pile Gully scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pikes Creek or Pile Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pile Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
